types: from psycopg2 import DatabaseError, IntegrityError, OperationalError

primary_operations.name: save
primary_operations.sdk_call: with self._conn.cursor() as cur: cur.execute('INSERT INTO entities(id, payload) VALUES (%s, %s) ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET payload = EXCLUDED.payload', (entity_id, payload)); self._conn.commit()

primary_operations.name: find_by_id
primary_operations.sdk_call: with self._conn.cursor() as cur: cur.execute('SELECT payload FROM entities WHERE id = %s', (entity_id,)); row = cur.fetchone(); return bytes(row[0])

primary_operations.name: delete
primary_operations.sdk_call: with self._conn.cursor() as cur: cur.execute('DELETE FROM entities WHERE id = %s', (entity_id,)); self._conn.commit()

code_style_notes: psycopg2 connections are NOT thread-safe; use a connection pool (psycopg2.pool) in concurrent code
use %s as the parameter placeholder; never %d or f-strings with user input
ON CONFLICT requires PostgreSQL >= 9.5; for older servers use UPDATE then INSERT pattern
